page: Amazon Web Services m8idn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized virtual machine instance. It is powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and features a dedicated Intel x86_64 processor. The instance provides 32 vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz.

The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, which translates to a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For high-speed local storage, the m8idn.8xlarge includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. This local storage configuration supports applications requiring low-latency, high-throughput data access.

Networking capabilities include a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps, optimized for both general network traffic and Elastic Block Store connectivity. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of high-speed dedicated Intel processing, substantial local NVMe storage, and high-capacity networking provides a dense resource profile suitable for demanding enterprise workloads without requiring separate storage-optimized or network-optimized instances.

The hardware configuration makes the m8idn.8xlarge well-suited for general-purpose enterprise applications, relational databases, caching layers, backend web serving, and data processing tasks that benefit from fast local instance storage and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idn.8xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel x86_64 processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. The system is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. For high-speed local storage, the instance includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. It also delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This combination of dedicated compute power, high-speed local storage, and high network throughput makes the m8idn.8xlarge highly efficient for resource-dense workloads. It is designed for enterprise applications, relational databases, caching layers, and data processing tasks requiring fast local storage and robust network performance.
